ID:       ddx-3892c623
Title:    Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part: part 3
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-130cf9ab
Desc:     PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
Accept:   1. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DDx has no runtime concept of a "global" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.
- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `<project>/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.
- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.
2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:
   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` if the dir exists → layer "project".
   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>` if it exists → layer "global".
   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer "baked-in"; return an error for any other unresolved name.
3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).
- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:56:37.269486624Z"}

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS

`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. - 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.

(Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS

This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
   a. ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> if it exists -> scope 'project'.
   b. GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> if it exists -> scope 'global'.
   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -> scope 'baked-in'.
   d. otherwise ok=false.
3. Leave FEAT-015 ~-target rejection in installer.go untouched.

NON-SCOPE: wiring --global into install/update/init/doctor (sibling children); writing/symlinking the global tree.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4675-4701` currently creates each child with `Parent: parent.ID` and then explicitly adds dependency edges from the parent to every child. The static prompt text in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2490-2577` and the guardrail snapshots in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go` still teach the decomposition flow to use the parent as a dependency edge, so the bug survives both the runtime path and the emitted instructions.

PROPOSED FIX
Change the programmatic decomposition path and the tracked prompt/guardrail text so generated children never add the parent to their deps. Keep only legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges. Add a golden e2e test named `decompose_does_not_self_dep` that creates a parent with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.

NON-SCOPE

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go

ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder once failed to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when readiness_checks[].verdict arrived as a JSON bool, because the producer (readiness prompt) emitted bool while the consumer (Go decoder) parsed string. Under warn-only policy every claim attempt produced an identical-fingerprint intake_error warn and the worker idled the whole fizeau queue (23 ready beads, one daemon) indefinitely. The detail string was: "decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string". This child owns the schema-gate slice of parent ddx-61f6c57d: make the readiness payload shape a single enforced contract so this class of producer/consumer drift fails the build instead of silently wedging the queue at runtime.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict shape contract lives in two places that can drift independently: the producer prompt string assembled in buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639-642) and the Go consumer readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125), which coerces bool true->"pass", bool false->"fail", string->lowercased, null/absent->"". The canonical shape is declared in cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json (readiness_checks[].verdict is oneOf boolean/string/null). Without a CI gate binding all three together, a change to any one side reintroduces the wedge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth for the readiness payload. Ensure (a) the schema accepts exactly the verdict forms readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON accepts, (b) the producer prompt continues to cite readinessChecksSchemaPath (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21,639-642) as authoritative, and (c) a CI gate fails on drift: the `readiness-schema` Makefile target (cli/Makefile:152-156) runs the schema tests and is invoked from .github/workflows/ci.yml:66-67 via `make readiness-schema`. The gate must fail if the schema, the prompt, or the decoder diverge on the verdict contract.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the same-fingerprint warn escalation / silent-idle guard — that is the sibling child (worker loop escalation). Do not change the warn-only policy_mode semantics. Do not implement any other parent acceptance criterion. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint.","events_attachment":"ddx-1d071ef5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1d071ef5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","decomposed-child","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-500716b9","status":"closed","title":"Enforce shared readiness_checks schema gate in CI so producer prompt and Go decoder cannot drift on verdict type","updated_at":"2026-05-28T04:00:21.041894511Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-28T01:46:59Z","count":3,"endpoint":"http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions","model":"qwen3.5-27b","provider":"bragi","reason":"provider_connectivity","timeout_class":"bad gateway"}],"work-no-changes-count":2}

`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. .ddx/beads.jsonl:87:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead.*Cleanup|TestCleanupAttemptWorktree' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.
3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).
4. A unit test asserts `ddx try --no-merge` preserves the worktree regardless of outcome.
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"e979bcd786ff98bd4767596dd1ed2dbe76cddb0c","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:02:14.061932044Z","description":"PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success
outcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,
`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),
its execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attemptID>/`
is not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*
is launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree
`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+
hours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).
The bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new
execute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits
from the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that
bead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.

The user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just
on the one observed bead.

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`
defines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring "the parent's responsibility —
call it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous
attempts do not accumulate." The function matches strictly by
`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"` and is only invoked
on a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:

  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree
      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).
      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.
  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never
      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve
      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to
      the original).

The post-attempt outcome classifier at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:
task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place
to call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.
The existing landing path at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls
`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but
those are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead
worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome
classification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the
per-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than
`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:
  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve
     for inspection regardless of outcome).
  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the
     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call
     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).

Implementation steps the executing agent should follow:
  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the
     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook
     point.
  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,
     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for
     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.
  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.
  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success
     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the
     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).
  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a
     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers
  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).
- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale
  worktrees (potential follow-up).
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.
- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add
  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on
  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct
  and untouched.
- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.
- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead

.ddx/beads.jsonl:91:{"acceptance":"1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:94:{"acceptance":"1. Child beads are filed for: diagnose function, RC-1 classifier fix, RC-2 supersession cascade, RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup, RC-6 auto-epic-decompose, idle-path wiring, work focus two-section output, work plan --explain/--json flags.
2. After all children close, running ./bin/ddx work --once against the current repo state auto-closes the 6 mislabeled closure-candidate epics (ddx-6862f2ac, -83440482, -ca1c66ee, -cc0641a7, -ccda7a32, -eeced112), auto-closes ddx-847df529 via supersession cascade, and dispatches at most one auto-decompose against any genuinely-undecomposed epic.
3. After a single ddx work --once cycle, ddx bead status shows non-zero Ready or Worker ready, OR ddx work focus Section A surfaces the remaining beads with concrete reason codes from the FEAT-010 taxonomy.
4. ddx work plan --explain prints a per-bead reason code for every non-ready entry; no entry reports no_diagnosis.
5. ddx work --once --no-auto-epic-decompose --no-auto-supersede-close --no-auto-closure-reclassify reproduces today's "skipped N ready epic(s)" behavior, proving the kill switches work.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green after all child beads land.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-16T16:55:34.976453964Z","description":"PROBLEM
ddx work currently emits "No execution-eligible beads in the queue" and exits when the only ready beads are epics with open children, mislabeled closure candidates, superseded leftovers, dead intermediates, or epic-of-epic chains. Operators have to manually triage every blocker class. This violates the factory rule that ddx work must self-unstick (reliability principle P10, just added).

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-167 (post-edit) and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md "Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation" subsection (just added) now mandate diagnose+auto-remediate semantics. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today only reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, then sleeps/exits — no diagnoser, no remediation dispatch. cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:218 isOrdinaryEpicContainer conflates openChildCount with totalChildCount, mislabeling 6 of 10 ready epics in the current project as containers when they are actually closure candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Build the diagnose+auto-remediate idle path per FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation: per-bead Diagnose function with the closed reason taxonomy; remediator handlers for RC-1 (lifecycle classifier fix), RC-2 (supersession cascade in Close), RC-3 (dead-intermediate cleanup), RC-6 (auto-epic-decompose wrapping runPreClaimDecomposer); idle-path wiring in execute_bead_loop.go that runs diagnoser, fires matching remediators, re-scans without sleeping on success; operator surface (ddx work focus) two-section output that NEVER silently hides auto-remediable beads; ddx work plan --explain/--json flags for inspection. All safety guards (attempt cap, cost budget, cooldown, cycle guard, operator overrides, audit events) per the spec.

NON-SCOPE
- Building the FEAT-004 epic-scoped worker mode (dedicated branch/worktree, sequential child execution). Explicitly deferred in the FEAT-004 update.
- Replacing the operator-invoked bead-breakdown skill. It stays the right tool for top-down planning from a fresh spec/plan.
- Two-worker concurrency redesign — current tracker serialization is reused.

CHILD QUEUE
- diagnose: per-bead reason classifier (cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go)
- RC-1: classifier split openChildCount/totalChildCount (cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go)
- RC-2: supersession cascade in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-3: dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-6: auto-epic-decompose handler (cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go)
- idle-path wiring: diagnose+dispatch+rescan in execute_bead_loop.go:1263
- work focus: two-section output (cli/cmd/work_focus.go)
- work plan: --explain/--json flags (cli/cmd/work_plan.go)","id":"ddx-2a1ece2b","issue_type":"epic","labels":["kind:epic","area:agent","area:cli","area:bead-lifecycle","phase:2","operator-ux","spec:FEAT-004","spec:FEAT-010","spec:reliability-principles"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"epic: ddx work — idle-path diagnose and auto-remediate (factory mode)","updated_at":"2026-05-16T16:55:34.976453964Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install <name>` in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install <name>` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.
3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8fbb32ad36b842cefed25f048cd5511a8b64afe9","created_at":"2026-06-03T11:21:13.13129759Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)

Without `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`<project>/.claude/skills` and `<project>/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.

ROOT CAUSE
- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `<workingDir>/.agents/skills` and `<workingDir>/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `<project>/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.
- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.
- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name>` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`; in convention mode (no `<project>/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).
- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

The pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.

ROOT CAUSE
- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes "Canonical schema: "+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.
- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.
- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true->pass, bool false->fail, string->lowercased, null/absent->empty).
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.
