A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"13eaa6a7758297f2001118d4533a33c70a272e9d","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.026972073Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Plugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.
- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure `ResolvePlugin` returns (path, layer, err) with layer ∈ {project, global, baked-in} and the exact precedence project → global → baked-in.
2. Add table-driven tests in internal/registry/resolve_test.go pinning both the prefers-project and falls-back-to-global behaviors against a temp HOME/XDG fixture.
3. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" three-layer precedence note.","events_attachment":"ddx-3478153c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3478153c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e6291b32","status":"closed","title":"registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:23:53.361549509Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. The execution/runtime packages still build project-state paths with literal `.ddx` joins, so run-state, evidence, worker liveness, and exec attachments will not follow convention-mode roots even after the CLI layer is fixed.

ROOT CAUSE
Representative direct joins remain in `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:57`, `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:216`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1790`, `cli/internal/exec/store.go:54`, `cli/internal/exec/bead_runtime.go:421`, `cli/internal/workerstatus/liveness.go:42`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:667`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go:235`, and `cli/internal/persona/loader.go:77`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add package-local helpers that route runtime/project-state paths through `ddxroot.Path` for `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, `internal/persona`, `internal/workerstatus`, `internal/processmetrics`, and `internal/agentmetrics`.
- Replace the remaining literal `.ddx` joins in those packages.
- Add focused tests proving run-state/evidence and exec attachment paths resolve against the DDx root chosen by `ddxroot.Path`.

NON-SCOPE
- `cli/cmd`, config/git, and server/graphql packages.
- Test-only files and the final full-tree hook gate.

There is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).
- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.

The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.

reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping

Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.

The lifecycle dispatch path still returns the generic `project root mutation rejected` error and the caller path continues to log it as a warning/continuing condition. When that happens in the same run that already exhibits a parent-back-edge state, the operator only sees a soft warning instead of the structural failure that actually occurred.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:130-165` rejects root mutations but does not include the offending bead ID in the failure shape, and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:595` plus the surrounding loop code consume the error as a warning path. The result is a muted signal even though the run is already in a bad structural state.

PROPOSED FIX
When the parent-back-edge state and a root-mutation rejection coincide, upgrade the log/event severity from warning to error and attach the offending bead ID to the emitted message. Add the regression `TestDispatchLogsErrorOnRootMutationWithBackEdge` so the severity change cannot drift.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the store validator, doctor scan/repair, or decomposition prompt text in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go

During live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Plain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. The intended model is that project init only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and the project manifest; plugins should lazy-resolve from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` currently routes plain init through eager plugin setup.
- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 calls `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)` unconditionally, producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` in the project.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Remove the default plugin install from the no-flag `ddx init` path.
2. Keep project-local initialization of `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest intact.
3. Ensure the no-flag path does not create or modify any project `plugins/` tree or agent-tier skill links.
4. Leave actual plugin lookup to `registry.ResolvePlugin`, preferring project over global, from the sibling registry-resolver bead.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- sibling registry-resolver bead.

PARENT
- ddx-937de9fc.","events_attachment":"ddx-42c2c630/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-42c2c630","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-64ecd687","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-361c4b9b","status":"closed","title":"init: stop project-local default plugin installation in plain ddx init","updated_at":"2026-06-02T05:25:43.926200049Z"}

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Hidden lifecycle model calls in `ddx work --watch` can mutate the operator's project checkout before a bead attempt starts. Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.
`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.

PROPOSED FIX
Run all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.","events_attachment":"ddx-492050f3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-492050f3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execution","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","dirty-tree","principle:isolation"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f4aa254d","status":"closed","title":"work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root","updated_at":"2026-05-15T16:36:24.296964643Z"}

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
- `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
- Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.

Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-49737227/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-49737227","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:cleanup","decomposed"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-c191e91a","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-179843a5","status":"closed","title":"housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 1","updated_at":"2026-05-15T06:35:44.613080731Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T06:09:55.99701302Z"}

When pre_claim_intake produces a warn-only rejection with the same fingerprint repeatedly (for example a readiness schema mismatch), the worker logs a warning and goes idle every cycle (pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle). With many ready beads and one daemon, none get claimed and the worker looks alive while making zero progress, leaving the queue idle indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE
- warn-only policy mode masks the failure: the loop emits pre_claim_intake.warn at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1859, :1982, :2009, :2034, :2073, each carrying a fingerprint computed in cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-24, and then goes loop.idle (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1637-1649) with no guard counting consecutive identical fingerprints across distinct bead IDs to break the silent loop.
- The threshold knob exists at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:76-79 (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero means DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) and the escalation message format at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1141.

PROPOSED FIX
After N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason "preclaim_warn_repeated", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"bd11f3c9adebedc32b3639a97362f4129071bab6","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.360272672Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

First-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus "lazy-resolves-to-global" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the "Global Install (...) — <status>" and "Project Install (...) — <status>" lines (doctor.go:447-448).
- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.
3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing" and "Project Install (<projectRoot>/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global".
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-03T02:46:46.787576605Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:175:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.","created_at":"2026-06-01T21:09:49.582933254Z","description":"**The bug**

`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in
one of two locations based on whether `<projectRoot>/.ddx/` exists as a
directory:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
        return inTree    // <projectRoot>/.ddx/
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>
}
```

The `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named
`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home
directory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's
where user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the
parallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made
with `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`
override), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a
project root.

Result: the same logical state files appear at two different paths
depending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.
The concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS
cert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`
(via systemd) wrote its cert at
`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate
invocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote
`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same
filename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some
other client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got
`tls: bad certificate`.

**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**

`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites
across the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The
highest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT
diverge between locations):

- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,
  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,
  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)
- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)
- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)
- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)
- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)
- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)
- `workers/<id>/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,
  `executions_mirror.go:211`)
- `executions/<attempt-id>/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,
  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)
- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)
- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,
  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)
- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)
- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)
- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)
- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)

Anything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`
arguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.

**Proposed fix (structural)**

Tighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but
not sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization
marker: `<projectRoot>/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by
`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir
        return inTree
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root
}

func isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {
    info, err := os.Stat(dir)
    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
        return false
    }
    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "config.yaml"))
    return err == nil
}
```

`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.

This makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to
`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>` (since `~/.ddx/` has no
`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.

**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**

A surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`
tests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare
`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old "any `.ddx/` = project root"
contract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`
run:

- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,
  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)

Files that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`
in the `.ddx/`:

- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)
- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`
- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`
- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`
- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)

A central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)
that creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.

**Migration**

Existing user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.
gets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`
returned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition
is graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but
operators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot
migration. Recommend:

1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the
   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.
2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host-id>/server/`
   on first server start under the new resolver.

**Operational note**

The original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the
entire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir
cert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.
That's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.

**Severity: P1**

The bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as
"server unreachable" rather than "bead tracker corrupted" is that
nobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files
recently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses
`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test
   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`
   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.
2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write
   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).
3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.
4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /
   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.
5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.

**Out of scope**

- Renaming `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/` (separate cleanup).
- Multi-project federation collision (different concern, different
  layer).
- The TLS-renew schedule / rotation logic (orthogonal — the bug is
  not "the cert was rotated"; it's "two carts exist").","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/` without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.","coverage":"Child 1 AC 1 covers `TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`; Child 1 AC 4 covers `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/ddxroot/...`."},{"parent_ac":"2. Runtime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `"baked-in"` with an empty path only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>`.
- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `"project"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).
- Guarantee it returns layer `"global"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.
- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name=="ddx"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.
- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.","events_attachment":"ddx-536c3dd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-536c3dd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b67715d8","status":"closed","title":"registry: ResolvePlugin honors project→global→baked-in precedence with table tests","updated_at":"2026-06-03T07:21:01.247260548Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:187:{"acceptance":"1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with exported Path(ctx, projectRoot) function.
2. TestDDxRoot_DefaultsToProjectRootDotDDx asserts Path returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx") in the default (no XDG fallback yet) case.
3. Static check: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. This is the regression guard for the audit. (The check is wired into lefthook as a pre-commit rule.)
4. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. All existing tests pass without modification.
5. The audit covers at minimum the 12 path-writers enumerated in the description. The bead's no_changes_rationale.txt (if used) MUST list every file modified by the audit, grouped by package.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes, including the new "no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths" rule.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:08:53.863138043Z","description":"PROBLEM
Every place in the codebase that writes to .ddx/<something> today hardcodes filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...). This makes it impossible to relocate the DDx state to an XDG location for convention-mode projects without auditing and rewriting every call site. A leaked path means the convention-mode invariant ("no files written to project tree without a .ddx/ folder") breaks the moment that code path runs.

The full enumeration of touched paths is at minimum:
- .ddx/config.yaml
- .ddx/beads.jsonl
- .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
- .ddx/attachments/<id>/
- .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
- .ddx/run-state/<id>.json
- .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
- .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
- .ddx/plugins/<name>/
- .ddx/workers/<id>/
- .ddx/agent-logs/
- .ddx/scratch/
- and likely 10+ more across cli/internal/server/, cli/cmd/, library/checks/, scripts/

ROOT CAUSE
There is no single accessor that returns "the DDx root for this project". cli/internal/config/ has GetMetaPrompt() and similar config getters, but no DDxRoot(ctx) function. Every path-writer reaches into the file system directly.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a new package cli/internal/ddxroot with a single public function: `Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string`. Today returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"); a follow-up bead (E2) extends it to fall back to XDG when projectRoot/.ddx does not exist.
2. Audit every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment. Replace `filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...)` with `filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), ...)`. The audit must cover cli/cmd/, cli/internal/, library/checks/ (where applicable to ddx-side paths).
3. Add a build-time linter check (or a pre-commit hook lint rule) that fails the build if a new `filepath.Join.*"\\.ddx"` literal appears outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Use go vet via a custom analyzer OR a simple ripgrep-based pre-commit hook (lefthook).
4. The resolver MUST NOT change behaviour in this bead: for any projectRoot today, ddxroot.Path returns exactly filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"). This bead is a pure refactor — no functional change.
5. Existing tests must continue to pass without modification. If any test fails, it has hardcoded .ddx in a way that bypassed the resolver — fix that test.

NON-SCOPE
- XDG fallback path (E2 bead).
- Convention mode bootstrap (E2 bead).
- `ddx init --global` (E3 bead).
- Manifest overlay (E5 bead).
- Migration of existing data (none required; behaviour is identical).

DEPS
None. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. 4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3946d29110f96ee9d380f6ad28f88ab1fa4fb526","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.178056222Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-07af62e6","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-42c2c630","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.
- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).
2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.
3. init (no flag): create `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
