.ddx/beads.jsonl:212:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 3 verifiable AC items: unit tests covering the four classification scenarios; lefthook green; manual smoke confirming worker emits 'server unreachable' instead of flagging beads when server is down.","created_at":"2026-06-01T01:13:59.24088712Z","description":"**Problem**

When `ddx work --watch` runs against a `ddx server` that is partially
unavailable (e.g. TLS handshake failure, agent-service connection
refusal, persistent route-resolution failures with no candidates), the
worker treats every individual bead as the failure unit. Each attempt
gets flagged with `operator_attention` via the progress-watchdog or
`execution_failed` via the routing preflight, then the next bead is
tried, then the next.

Result: a single server-side problem (one bad TLS cert, an expired
provider credential, a stale model catalog) silently turns the entire
ready queue into "needs human" beads, even though the *beads* are fine
— the *server* is broken. The worker keeps idling, the operator sees
"0 closures in 24h" with no clear signal that this is environmental.

Observed concretely on niflheim on 2026-05-31:

- `ddx server` was serving a TLS cert from
  `$DDX_PROJECT_ROOT/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` (Apr 17 fingerprint)
  while the client `ddx work` pinned the cert in `~/.ddx/server/tls/`
  (May 18 fingerprint). The server logged
  `http: TLS handshake error from 127.0.0.1:XXXXX: remote error: tls:
  bad certificate` every ~5 min for ~36 hours.
- Every `niflheim-71365a9f` attempt during that window failed with
  `ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 16 candidates rejected,
  outcome_reason=no_viable_provider`.
- After 15+ attempts the worker flagged the bead for operator
  attention, then quietly idled with `execution-ready=0 blocked=47
  operator-attention=17 needs-human/investigation=10`. No clear
  signal to the operator that the *server* was the cause.

**Desired behavior**

The worker should detect server-level failures separately from
bead-level failures. Two-tier classification:

1. **Bead-level failure** (current behavior): the bead's tests/lint
   fail, the agent commits something invalid, etc. → mark the bead
   `execution_failed`, retry per policy, eventually
   `operator-attention`.
2. **Server-level failure** (new): TLS handshake error, agent-service
   connection refused, persistent `no_viable_provider` across ≥ K
   distinct beads in the last M minutes (default K=3, M=10) → mark
   the *server* unavailable, NOT the individual beads. Loop into a
   probe-and-resume mode: every 30 s ping
   `https://server/api/health`; the moment the server returns 200
   and a small `ddx run --text` smoke test succeeds, re-enter the
   queue. None of the beads attempted during the server-failure
   window get penalized.

The server-failure backoff prevents the per-bead operator-attention
flagging storm we observed. It also makes the actual root cause
visible — `ddx work` should emit a single distinctive log line
("server unreachable: holding queue until /api/health returns") and
expose that state via `ddx work status` so the operator notices it.

**In-scope files (likely; verify before edit)**

- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` — wraps `ResolveRoute` →
  classification site for routing failures.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — the place where
  `no_viable_provider` is turned into `outcome_reason` for the bead.
- The watcher loop in `cli/internal/work/` (or wherever `ddx work
  --watch` lives) — needs the new server-unavailable state machine.
- `cli/internal/agent/` — health-probe helper.
- New unit tests covering: (a) K consecutive `no_viable_provider`
  across distinct bead IDs trips server-failure mode; (b)
  server-failure backoff doesn't penalize the beads it was about to
  try; (c) successful health probe transitions back to normal queue
  drain; (d) TLS handshake error from the server is classified as
  server-level regardless of bead.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./cli/internal/agent/... ./cli/internal/work/...` passes
   with the new tests above.
2. `lefthook run pre-commit` (or equivalent gates) green.
3. Manual smoke: stop `ddx server`, run `ddx work --watch`; observe
   the worker emits "server unreachable" within 30 s and does NOT
   flag any beads for operator attention while the server is down.
   Restart `ddx server`; observe queue drain resumes.

**Out of scope**

- Auto-repairing server-side issues (TLS cert refresh, credential
  rotation). Operator still has to fix the server. The worker just
  has to detect and not amplify the failure.
- Multi-server failover.
- Changing the existing per-bead `operator-attention` semantics —
  those stay for true bead-level failures.

**Severity**: P1. Prevents quiet queue-stall pattern that wastes
operator hours on triage when the actual cause is environmental.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness route unavailable: lifecycle dispatch: project root mutation rejected for bead ddx-658cb394 (new dirty paths: .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl)","fingerprint":"95e97863a4f28de5ac8d57b9653dafd02af55ab5eadad1606ae82f0cd215b715","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"95e97863a4f28de5ac8d57b9653dafd02af55ab5eadad1606ae82f0cd215b715"}","created_at":"2026-06-07T08:19:26.808156309Z","kind":"intake.warn","source":"ddx work","summary":"system_unready"}],"id":"ddx-658cb394","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:work","area:agent","kind:reliability","kind:operator-experience"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"ddx work: detect server-level failures separately from bead-level (don't flag beads when server is unavailable)","updated_at":"2026-06-07T08:19:26.85968905Z","work-last-detail":"tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)","work-last-status":"preclaim-hook-failed","work-retry-after":"2026-06-06T09:50:22Z"}

PROBLEM: Decomposition can still produce child beads whose deps include the parent when agent-facing instructions or structured decomposition payloads request `child -> parent` edges. That recreates the deadlock even if the direct `dep add` path is fixed only later or if another writer bypasses the CLI.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: The execute-bead prompt snapshots instruct agents to run `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` at `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt:18` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt:16`. The preclaim readiness payload model includes suggested child `Parent` and `Deps` fields at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:259`, but the decomposition application path at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4678` only creates children and wires parent deps, with no golden assertion that children exclude the parent from deps.

PROPOSED FIX: Update live prompt construction and checked-in prompt snapshots so decomposition instructions create children with `parent=<parent-id>` and wire only `parent -> child` dependencies. Add validation in the programmatic decomposition path that rejects or strips any suggested child dep equal to the parent or an ancestor before tracker mutation. Add a golden e2e test that decomposes a bead into N children and asserts every child has `Parent == parent.ID` and no dependency target equal to the parent.

NON-SCOPE: Do not implement the global `DepAdd` invariant here except where needed by decomposition writer tests. Do not add doctor repair. Do not change post-attempt remediation or operator-attention behavior. Do not relax AC-map completeness rules.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"5843edc5ebcb5f64b14cae384b8f22dbb6bdbea5","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.50344695Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

First-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `<project>/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).
- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (<globalPath>) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (<projectPath>) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.
2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-67b477d3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-67b477d3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-524be1ab","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers","updated_at":"2026-06-03T16:48:57.361307585Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:220:{"acceptance":"`ddx install --force --local <path>` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.","closing_commit_sha":"08558b9bffef71ec63fdf2485ee4f4d054d8c166","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205231666Z","description":"## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

`ddx install <plugin> --force --local <repo>` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.

## Reproduction

1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)
2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)
3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`
4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`

Repro on HELIX:
```
ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix
# Error: validating package structure:
#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...
```

Workaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.

## Expected behavior

`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v <path>` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.

## Related

- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.
- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.","events_attachment":"ddx-686564bf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-686564bf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:install","area:validation","observed-failure"],"owner":"easel","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eec2ad7d","status":"closed","title":"ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs","updated_at":"2026-05-27T03:18:37.806916401Z"}

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

PARENT AND DEPS

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4ac5ec29273c18d7542398a829434fd1683b8ee6","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.38876229Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Operators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).
- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.
- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name> --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).
2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.
3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.
4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works".
5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-6931d485/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6931d485","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-93a1e107","status":"closed","title":"install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:16:46.067151221Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:228:{"created_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z","description":"PROBLEM
The docker-clone attempt backend is unusable on a remote, rootless, or de-nested Docker daemon (e.g. OrbStack on this machine, docker info Name=orbstack). Every attempt fast-fails in 17-39ms before any agent work with: docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist: <host path>. Outcome is classified execution_failed / transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:612 dockerRunArgs() constructs the container with five classes of HOST bind-mount (--mount type=bind,src=<hostpath>):
  - :622 src=ws.WorkDir            dst=/work               (cloned repo)
  - :623 src=exe                   dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx  (ddx binary, os.Executable())
  - :636 src=ws.DockerRun          dst=/ddx-runtime        (HOME/cache/tmp + injected creds)
  - :641-642 gocache, work-tmp                              (Go build/test cache)
  - :644-649 dockerToolMounts() -> claude/codex/gemini      (agent CLIs via exec.LookPath)
A de-nested/remote daemon's filesystem namespace does not include this machine's /home/erik/... , so every src path 'does not exist' from the daemon's view. Confirmed: 4 attempts on ddx-b5816493 failed this way; failing paths included /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx and /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145. A short -v probe 'succeeded' only because docker silently auto-created an empty dir (the classic -v footgun), proving the source was not visible.

PROPOSED FIX (decomposed into child tasks)
Adopt the standard create -> cp -> start -> cp-out -> rm pattern used for remote/rootless daemons, plus bake static tooling into the image and use daemon-managed named volumes for caches. Only the daemon's own storage (image layers + named volumes) is reliably reachable; everything dynamic is copied in/out.
  1. Bake agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image; drop their bind-mounts.
  2. Keystone: move workspace + ddx binary in via docker create/cp instead of bind.
  3. Convert Go caches + runtime dir to named volumes.
  4. Inject credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind.

NON-SCOPE
- The worktree and local-clone backends (unaffected; worktree is the working default).
- Routing/provider selection.

NOTE: filed from an operator session after switching the live queue drain to --attempt-backend worktree to work around this. Child tasks carry their own file:line scope and AC.","id":"ddx-6967f6e6","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","reliability","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"docker-clone attempt backend: eliminate host bind-mounts for remote/de-nested daemon compatibility","updated_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z"}

Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.
- Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"299098ab440486a2d72db530d3eae213925c1c19","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:42:23.121155693Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.
- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.
- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx update`. When set, update only the global plugin tree under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>; do not touch the project tree.
2. Without `--global`, `ddx update` updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree <project>/.ddx/plugins or convention-mode XDG project plugins).
3. `ddx doctor` reports both layers: a "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" line and a "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global" line, using the tier-resolution info exposed by the registry bead.
4. Land the spec/docs updates: file-or-update the skill-install-topology ADR (ADR-025 or the equivalent skill-topology record), document install locations in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", document agent-tier paths in library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md if that file exists, and document the install precedence in the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section.

NON-SCOPE
- install / init `--global` flags (sibling beads).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + tier reporting) — update and doctor consume it.

After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
- TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.

NON-SCOPE

ddx ships both `ddx install <name>` and `ddx plugin install <name>` for the same operation. FEAT-001-cli.md:102 (item 38) names `ddx plugin install <name>` as the canonical surface. The top-level `ddx install` is a vestige that survived the move to project-local plugins (CLAUDE.md:30-33 and engineering-principles.md:18,21 still mention it). Users get duplicate help paths, output messages point at the wrong surface, and the help text itself shows examples that mix both forms.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586 registers `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` (top-level). cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 newPluginCommand reuses the SAME command object via `installCmd := f.newInstallCommand(); installCmd.Use = "install <name>"; cmd.AddCommand(installCmd)`. Both surfaces resolve to the same code; only the help text differs. Output messages and docs that should point users at `ddx plugin install` still point at `ddx install`:
- cli/cmd/list.go:245 — "(none — run 'ddx install <name>' to install)"
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:464 — "Run 'ddx install %s' to update."
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:839 — "MISSING (%s not found, run: ddx install %s)"
- cli/cmd/init.go:111 — "ddx install helix   - Install HELIX workflow (optional)"
- cli/cmd/install.go:31-34 — Long help body for the cmd (examples list)
- cli/cmd/install.go:974 — error string "run 'ddx install <name> --force' to repair"
- CLAUDE.md:30-33 — mentions `ddx install <name>` as a current surface
- docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21 — same
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194 — same

Tests that shell `ddx install ...` directly:
- cli/cmd/installation_acceptance_test.go:361,760,870 — exercise `ddx install --offline ...` (verify behavior; rewrite to `ddx plugin install --offline` if --offline is preserved)
- cli/cmd/install_manifest_test.go:668 — asserts the removed `--global` flag still errors (rewrite to call `ddx plugin install --global` and assert the same error)

PROPOSED FIX
Single-PR change:
1. Remove `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` at cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586. Keep newInstallCommand() the constructor since cli/cmd/install.go:159 newPluginCommand still calls it. Optionally rename to newPluginInstallCommand for clarity.
2. Update newPluginCommand at cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 to also override the Long help body so it no longer references "ddx install" examples (it currently inherits the parent Long with mixed examples).
3. Update the 4 user-facing output messages in cli/cmd/{list.go:245, command_factory.go:464, doctor.go:839, init.go:111, install.go:974} to say `ddx plugin install ...`.
4. Update docs that name `ddx install` as a current surface: CLAUDE.md:30-33, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21, docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194. Replace with `ddx plugin install`.
5. Update the two tests cited above to invoke `ddx plugin install ...`. Add a new TestDDxInstall_TopLevelCommandRetired that runs `ddx install whatever` and asserts an exit code != 0 with an error message pointing to `ddx plugin install`.
6. Run `make build && ddx --help` and verify `install` is no longer listed under top-level commands. Verify `ddx install foo` produces the typical cobra "unknown command" error.

NON-SCOPE
- `ddx installed` and `ddx uninstall` (cli/cmd/command_factory.go:587-588) are also vestiges per FEAT-001-cli.md:107 (item 42 says they were removed but they are still registered). File a separate follow-up bead for those; this bead is scoped strictly to `ddx install`.
- The --offline flag semantics, the --force flag, or any other install behavior. Only the command surface moves.
- Library-registry or plugin manifest changes.

PARENT AND DEPS
No parent. ddx work's idle path (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315) reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail and then sleeps or exits. There is no per-bead diagnosis of WHY each non-ready bead is non-ready, so the loop cannot fire targeted auto-remediations. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires a closed reason taxonomy with one code per non-ready bead.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnoser exists. ReadyExecutionBreakdown returns bucket lists (Epics, EpicClosureCandidates, DepWaiting, OperatorAttention) but does not annotate each entry with why-it-is-blocked detail at a granularity the work loop can switch on.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go with a pure function Diagnose(b Bead, childIndex map[string][]Bead, openChildIndex map[string][]Bead, depIndex map[string][]Dependency) BlockReason. BlockReason is a typed struct: { Code BlockReasonCode; Detail string; CitedIDs []string }. BlockReasonCode is a typed-string enum with exactly the codes listed in FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation (superseded_pending_close, closure_candidate_misclassified, dead_intermediate_all_children_closed, dead_intermediate_open_children_pending, epic_of_epic, dep_blocked_by, genuinely_needs_decomposition, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, stale_graph_index, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis). The function is pure — no I/O — and takes pre-built indexes so it can be called from both the loop and the CLI.

Detection rules per code are specified in the FEAT-010 table. For superseded_pending_close, read the bead's superseded-by field OR a superseded-by:<Y> label, then verify Y is closed in the index. For closure_candidate_misclassified, check IssueType==epic AND len(openChildIndex[b.ID])==0 AND len(childIndex[b.ID])>0. For genuinely_needs_decomposition, check IssueType==epic AND len(childIndex[b.ID])==0 AND no manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label. For dep_blocked_by, cite every open dep's ID and status. Match codes in the order they appear in the FEAT-010 table; first match wins.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring Diagnose into the work loop (separate bead).
- Inventing new reason codes beyond the FEAT-010 taxonomy.
- Mutations of any kind.

PARENT AND DEPS

Genuinely undecomposed epics (IssueType==epic, totalChildCount==0, no closure-pending state) need automated decomposition into child task beads so ddx work can drain them. Today the only decomposition path is operator-invoked (bead-breakdown skill) or post-TooLarge recovery (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go). FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires the work loop to auto-dispatch decomposition for this specific case (RC-6) with strict preconditions to avoid the v1-plan duplicate-child bug.

ROOT CAUSE
No handler exists that bridges the diagnoser's genuinely_needs_decomposition reason code to runPreClaimDecomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:48). The existing post-TooLarge hook (NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook at recovery_decompose.go:107) gates only on TooLarge failure class, not on idle-scan diagnosis.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exporting AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, runner, rcfg, projectRoot, beadID) (DecomposeResult, error). It calls runPreClaimDecomposer (reused unchanged) after asserting every precondition. Preconditions (each must hold; first failing precondition produces a typed error AND increments the per-bead auto_decompose_attempted event count):
- store.Get(beadID).IssueType == "epic"
- totalChildCount(beadID) == 0 (use the same index ReadyExecutionBreakdown uses)
- bead is not currently in the EpicClosureCandidate bucket
- bead has no open dep_blocked_by or closed_or_missing_parent diagnoses (call the new Diagnose function on the bead; if it returns either, return ErrDepsBlockDecompose)
- bead carries no label in {manual-hold, no-auto-decompose, container}
- bead description is non-empty AND contains PROBLEM AND PROPOSED FIX sections AND has at least one acceptance criterion AND has at least one label matching ^(spec|area):
- per-bead event count of auto_decompose_attempted < 3
- bead has no active execution cooldown
- (operator override --no-auto-epic-decompose checked at the caller, not here)

On success: parent epic's execution_eligible is set to false (mirrors recovery_decompose.go behavior), child beads are created via store.Create, the auto_decompose_attempted event is emitted with body {child_ids: [...], cost_usd: N}.

On failure: SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, now+15m, "auto_decompose_failed", reason, baseRev), emit auto_decompose_failed event. On the 3rd cumulative attempt failure also emit auto_remediation_exhausted so the diagnoser surfaces it.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring this into the work loop (separate bead).
- Changing the runPreClaimDecomposer prompt (reused as-is).
- Surfacing the operator override flag (work-loop bead handles flag plumbing).

PARENT AND DEPS

`~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attempt>/` per attempt
(FEAT-010 layer 2). For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,
JVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an
empty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold
build from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent
on this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no
statement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.

OBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):
- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the
  execute-bead worktree.
- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run
  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.
- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly
  starved by worker cargo build I/O.
- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at
  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.

ROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a
non-concern. The feature explicitly lists "workspace creation, bead
attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle" but lists no resource-budget
or cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,
no documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and
no operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in
`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these
into the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):

1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):
   - Add a "Worktree resource expectations" subsection acknowledging that
     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain
     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.
   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or
     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through
     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).
   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native
     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.

2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):
   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,
     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as
     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`
     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.
   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any
     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.

3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)
   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the
   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a
   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,
   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is
   acceptable.

4. **Update CLAUDE.md "Execution Evidence Convention" section** if needed
   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection
   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.

NON-SCOPE:
- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any
  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation
  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has
  a clear policy to refer to.
- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk
  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).
- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for
  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md
- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md
  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.
- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is

updated_by = EXCLUDED.updated_by\\\",\\n            \u0026[\\n                \u0026key.collection.as_str(),\\n                \u0026key.namespace.database,\\n ],\\n        ))?;\\n        Ok(versioned)\\n    }\\n\\n    fn get_collection_view(\\n        \u0026self,\\n        collection: \u0026CollectionId,\\n    ) -\u003e self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\\n///\\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n           \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\\\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n                    \\\"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\\\"\\n                )))\\n            })?;\\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n url(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n            \u0026self.url\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    struct TestStore {\\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\\n        _database: TestDatabase,\\n }\\n\\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\\n        fn deref_mut(\u0026mut self) -\u003e \u0026mut Self::Target {\\n            \u0026mut self.adapter\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\\n            .lock()\\n            .expect(\\\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\\\")\\n    }\\n\\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: \u0026str, reason: \u0026str) {\\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \\\"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003cTestDatabase\u003e {\\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. data.text: I’m adding a small in-process bead corpus cache now. The store currently reparses the whole JSONL file on every mutation and read; with the perf test’s alternating claim/heartbeat/event/release loop that keeps the lock held long enough to miss the budget.

The first cache patch didn’t apply cleanly because the file’s local context shifted. I’m re-reading the exact store sections and will patch them in smaller chunks so I don’t disturb unrelated behavior.

data.text:
Blocked before commit.

What I changed:
